description:
A geographic map showing three distinct areas related to the Natura 2000 (N2K) network, each composed of a light blue inner zone and a surrounding peach-coloured buffer zone. The map illustrates how Natura 2000 site boundaries are used to define an Area of Interest (AoI), including a 2km buffer zone around selected sites.
Two main contiguous areas are visible. The upper left area is labelled "AT3210001". Below it, a connected area is labelled "IT3110017". To the right, another distinct area is also labelled "AT3210001". A smaller, isolated buffered area is partially visible at the bottom of the map, but its label is unreadable. The "AT" prefix indicates Austria, and "IT" indicates Italy, suggesting cross-border or nearby sites. The map visually demonstrates the N2K AoI creation process, highlighting instances where site definitions and buffer applications might lead to deviations from strict 2km buffer zones, as described in the accompanying text.
